title: Richly Buttery Norwegian Buns Bao
description: Buttery and springy Norwegian buns perfect for a sick day, featuring a unique dehydrating step and kosher-friendly ingredients, all cooked to perfection in a wok.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine yeast, sugar, and warm water, stirring to dissolve the yeast, then let it sit for 5 minutes.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and the yeast mixture.
Add the melted butter, egg, milk, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the wok over medium heat and add a small amount of oil to the bottom.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces, shaping each into a ball.
Dehydrate the buns in the wok for 5 minutes on each side, or until they are lightly browned and dry.
Brush the tops of the buns with additional melted butter and serve warm.
